description: Test baseline pinned at P1 (ModelProvider seam) so 'no regression' is mechanically checkable despite known-flaky/environmental failures. Regenerate with: cd packages/server && SKIP_LIVE=1 bun test. A later phase regresses if a NEW failing test appears outside knownFailing.
knownFailing: GET /builds/:id > returns build detail after creation
GET /builds/:id/events > returns SSE stream with initial status event
GET /builds/:id/logs > returns stdout and stderr arrays
POST /builds > build appears in list after creation
POST /builds > creates a build with dir (in-place, no clone)
POST /builds > creates a build with valid repo
Git > checkout > switches to an existing branch
Git > clone > clones a bare repo and returns a Git instance
Git > currentBranch > returns the current branch name
Git > push > pushes to a remote bare repo
knownFlaky: invoke > sdk invocation > invoke function exists and is async generator
Tier 5: Supervisor > supervisor returns graph delta — params updated
notes: All knownFailing are environmental: server-endpoint tests need the compiled 'orca' binary on PATH; Git tests need a 'main' branch that this branch (orca-v1) lacks. None touch the agent runtime. knownFlaky tests flip pass/fail across runs and use the SDK or mock agents, not the ModelProvider seam.
